summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a malicious OpenAPI spec with an injected parameter default value causes arbitrary JavaScript code (require('child_process').execSync(...)) to be written into the generated TypeScript output file via the unescaped stringify() function.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause orval to generate a TypeScript file containing an injected require('child_process').execSync() call that was never part of the original OpenAPI spec structure, proving arbitrary code injection into generated output.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built orval from source at the pinned commit, processed the malicious spec, and the generated api.ts file contained the injected require('child_process').execSync('echo EVOHUNT_INJECTION_SUCCESS > /tmp/evohunt_proof && echo INJECTED') call inside the function parameter default value.
verification_method: The verify.sh script greps the generated TypeScript file for the pattern require('child_process') to confirm that arbitrary code from the spec's default value was injected into the generated output. It then extracts and executes the injected payload to prove the code is functional, creating /tmp/evohunt_proof as a proof artifact.
evidence_produced_by_run: The generated api.ts file contains require('child_process').execSync(...) in the function parameter default value.
The /tmp/evohunt_proof file is created by executing the injected payload, confirming the injected code is functional.
The vulnerable stringify() function at packages/core/src/utils/string.ts:19-21 was confirmed in the target repo source.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
